Graph analytics of stock market

library(duckdb)
library(tidyverse)
library(tidyquant)

conn <- dbConnect(duckdb(), "data/sp500_prices_2026-04-03.duckdb", read_only = TRUE)
dbGetQuery(conn, "FROM prices LIMIT 3")
# dbGetQuery(conn, "select count(*) from prices;")
df_returns <- tbl(conn, "prices") |>
  as_tibble() |>
  group_by(symbol) |>
  tq_transmute(
    select = adjusted,
    mutate_fun = periodReturn,
    period = "daily",
    type = "log",
    leading = FALSE
  ) |>
  drop_na()
df_returns |> slice_head(n = 2)

Aggregate for undirected edges:

threshold for correlated stocks

Aggregate approach 1: Do not run

slow & memory hungry. archived as a reference.

cor_return12 <- function(x, y) {
  return(cor(x, y))
}

returns12 |>
  group_by(symbol1, symbol2) |>
  summarise(correlation = cor_return12(return1, return2))
